summary: The ticket contract is simpler and more trustworthy after this pass. Frontmatter now stays operational, durable links live in `Refs`, detailed proof lives in `Evidence`, and the tracked validator/runtime/doc surfaces agree with that rule.

rubric_sections.name: spec-contract
findings: The contract now gives one clear ownership rule: frontmatter for operational state, `Refs` for durable links, and `Evidence` for detailed proof.
The tracked ticket guidance prevents the template/docs from drifting back toward duplicated frontmatter/body ownership.

rubric_sections.name: debloatability
findings: The pass removes a duplicated frontmatter field from the canonical contract and stops repeating artifact-root guidance under multiple headings.
The added `tickets/AGENTS.md` rule makes the reduced shape easier to preserve during future ticket edits.

rubric_sections.name: integration-readiness
findings: The validator, stop-hook parser, ticket docs, and impl-plan references all encode the same reduced ticket contract.
Runtime-facing `ready` remains untouched, which keeps the cleanup scoped to real duplication instead of a broader state-model rewrite.

rubric_sections.name: evidence-quality
findings: The ticket links a fresh review artifact and records validator plus targeted unit-test results for the exact contract surfaces changed in this pass.
